---
output:
  xaringan::moon_reader:
    css: ["default", "extra.css"]
    lib_dir: libs
    seal: false
    nature:
      highlightStyle: github
      highlightLines: true
      countIncrementalSlides: false
      ratio: '16:9'
---

```{r, echo = FALSE, warning = FALSE, message = FALSE}
##xaringan::inf_mr()
## For offline work: https://bookdown.org/yihui/rmarkdown/some-tips.html#working-offline
## Images not appearing? Put images folder inside the libs folder as that is the main data directory

library(tidyverse)
library(readxl)
library(stargazer)
##library(kableExtra)
##library(modelr)

knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = FALSE,
                      eval = TRUE,
                      error = FALSE,
                      message = FALSE,
                      warning = FALSE,
                      comment = NA)
```

class: slideblue

.size80[**Today's Agenda**]

<br>

.size55[.center[
Explore real world cases of "green" subsidies addressing environmental problems
]]

<br>

<br>

.center[.size40[
  Justin Leinaweaver (Spring 2022)
]]

???

## Prep for Class
1. Review submitted cases



---

background-image: url('libs/Images/background-blue_triangles_v2.png')
background-size: 100%
background-position: center

class: middle

# Paper 3
.size40[
Propose a policy to address your selected environmental problem adapted to the specific features of your problem (Paper 1) and the complications you previously identified (Paper 2).

Support all claims with evidence (e.g. relevant academic literature or real-world examples of this kind of problem being addressed successfully)
]

???

Let's keep our focus on the task of this third section of our class.

Each week as we review each policy design option you need to make sure to keep your eye on the goal.

Keep asking yourself, how would I use this policy approach to address my chosen problem?

### Questions?




---

class: middle, slideblue

.size50[**Today: "Green" Subsidy Cases**]

.size40[
Find a real world example of this approach being used to address an environmental problem similar to the one in your project.

+ "Similar" in ANY of the specifics OR complications in your problem

+ What is the lesson for our new policy designs? 
]

???

### Does everybody have a case to present and discuss today?



---

background-image: url('libs/Images/12-1-Green_Subsidies_v2.png')
background-size: 80%
background-position: bottom
class: top, center

.size50[**Best advice / things to remember when designing "green" subsidies?**]

???

*ON BOARD*

Let's work together to build a policy cheat sheet on the board.

If you were designing a "green" subsidy to address an environmental problem what would you need to keep in mind?

- ?



---

background-image: url('libs/Images/13-1-fishing.jpg')
background-size: 100%
background-position: center
class: center, inverse

.textwhite[.size65[**Local Control of Common Pool Resources**]]

???

Next week we explore our next policy design, local control over shared natural resources!

Make sure you consolidate and clean up your notes from this week.

You might end up making this central to your paper!
